methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Rondo Energy, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by John O'Donnell and Eric Trusiewicz, has raised $150M+ to date. The valuation analysis benchmarks against a universe of 9 comparable companies—8 private and 1 public (LNZA)—all operating in the Climate & Energy sector at Series B stage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $150M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $285M. Peer-median analysis draws from six same-stage, same-sector comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and one additional private comparable—producing a median valuation of $355M. The peer range spans $120M to $5.0B, reflecting significant dispersion driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' outsized valuation.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $242M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $320M. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion—a 40x spread between minimum and maximum comps—and dependency on the $150M raised figure as a proxy for current valuation. The stage-progression method's reliance on standard multipliers may not capture Rondo's specific technology maturity or market position relative to the peer set.
